mission: Define the authoritative audit and WORM-ledger strategy so immutable audit truth, causality, disclosure posture, replay admissibility, and retention protection stay bound to one append-only audit spine from Phase 0 onward.
source_precedence: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay

defects.summary: Investigation export cannot claim completeness without the current graph snapshot and completeness verdict.
Retention preservation remains archive-only until the admissibility graph proves the exact preserved scope.
WORM and hash-chained audit classes may never enter ordinary deletion posture.
Break-glass review is still watch-bound while follow-up burden and reason adequacy remain open.
Recovery evidence remains watch-only until the graph refresh proves the latest rehearsal and continuity artifacts.

authoritativeAuditJoin.canonicalTruthRule: Server acceptance, local acknowledgement, and detached analytics may never replace the immutable audit join.

sampleAuditRecords.rationale: Patient callback establishes the first immutable join for the recovery shell.
Route intent becomes authoritative before any calm intake state can be trusted.
Transport acceptance is not authoritative; command ingest still binds the full audit join.
Command settlement distinguishes authoritative outcome from provisional acknowledgement.
Visible receipt proof and immutable audit now share the same causal token.
Heightened access stays immutable and remains tied to the exact shell and disclosure fence that widened scope.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
sampleAuditRecords.rationale: Replay cannot restore live support work until the same audit and completeness graph prove the chain.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
sampleAuditRecords.rationale: Restore settlement is authoritative only after the same immutable chain confirms replay-safe continuity.
Release publication is immutable but still watch-bound until the same continuity and assurance graph stay exact.
Freeze posture is stored as contract law, not as an ops-side deployment annotation.
Wave watch must reference the same continuity and admissibility proof as governance review.
Export stays blocked whenever the completeness graph cannot prove the exact admissible cut.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#13.3 Conservative retention freeze for high-risk episodes
sampleAuditRecords.rationale: Retention preservation blocks delete-ready posture until the same admissibility graph proves the scope and chain are complete.
Recovery evidence is recorded on the same audit spine but remains watch-bound until the evidence graph refresh completes.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
admissibilityDependencies.rationale: Audit search and timeline reconstruction consume the append-only audit chain plus the current admissibility graph rather than local joins.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
admissibilityDependencies.rationale: Support replay restore depends on the same immutable audit join, disclosure fence, and graph completeness verdict as export and investigation.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
admissibilityDependencies.rationale: Break-glass review remains watch-bound while reviewer burden is open, but still consumes the same audit spine and admissibility graph.

admissibilityDependencies.consumerLabel: Release watch and governance continuity pack
admissibilityDependencies.rationale: Governance review and ops watch stay on one continuity-proof loop and therefore remain watch-bound until the current evidence cut is exact.
Export cannot claim authoritative completeness without the same graph snapshot and verdict that govern replay and retention.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#13.3 Conservative retention freeze for high-risk episodes
admissibilityDependencies.rationale: Retention and freeze decisions must consume the same graph the audit, replay, export, and archive flows later validate.
Archive manifests are blocked until the same completeness verdict proves the preservation lineage is unsuperseded and in scope.
Delete-ready posture is forbidden for WORM and hash-chained audit classes even when later lifecycle automation exists.
Recovery evidence must stay on the same graph and audit spine as export, replay, and retention; stale graph refresh leaves it watch-bound.

graphAuthorities.purpose: authoritative admissibility graph for audit, replay, export, retention, and recovery completeness
fail-closed completeness verdict over the current admissible graph
